Quickly, Jacinto! We need to get out of here!
Meanwhile, inside the Sea of Stars control room...
Warning: hostile data detected in Reality Lens.
Warning: quarantine sequence could not be initiated.
Warning: processing load of defense systems is approaching critical levels. Risk of hostile data breach increasing.
Warning...
Warning sirens blared while the control room was flooded with error messages, painting it a strobing red.
Aaahhh! It's red... The screen has turned red!
Another Possibility.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Another Possibility.say: TB, what's happening? What do we do?
Another Possibility.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Another Possibility.say: The Galactic Core's systems are under attack by a hostile program.
Emergency quarantine measures have been initiated.
The attack is coming from within the Sea of Stars and is propagating at an extremely high rate. The defense systems will cease functioning shortly. Our only option is to initiate crisis mode.
Crisis... mode...?
Another Possibility.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Another Possibility.say: Affirmative. Please break the emergency glass on the third control panel.
Specifically, the glass panel with a red frame immediately under your right hand. Please break it and push the biggest button.
Red glass, red glass... There!
Another Possibility.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Another Possibility.say: Break the glass... Push the button... Okay!
Another Possibility.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Another Possibility.say: Orders received. Verifying credentials...
Credentials verified. Executing emergency orders and putting all systems in crisis mode.
Commencing hostile program countermeasures.

Turmoil and Ruin.say: Princeton and Laffey were still in the NY City simulation, only now the city had been so devastated as to render it unrecognizable.
The buildings on the horizon were burning and crumbling. This was the work of a black tornado, appearing suddenly and ravaging everything in mere minutes.
Did the Sea of Stars generate that black tornado, too?
Just for show? This IS a testing area, so it's not like it's any harm done...
Princeton, there are enemies coming toward us...
Huh... All I see is mist. I have no idea if those are ships or not.
Oh, wait, the Commander's files said something about this!
They were described as hostile entities that took the form of mist, appearing at the same time as a black tornado did.
No, but it sounds like that if you didn't read the files.
To put it simply, that mist is a projection of something or someone from a higher dimension.
They're not in our dimension, per se, which is why they look so hazy. It's also why we can't take measurements of them.
Got it. I'll be careful... Get ready to fight.

Engagement.say: But with these numbers... Plus, they show up out of nowhere and won't get off our tails.
It's okay. I'll deal with them somehow.

Harbingers.say: Sorry I couldn't catch up with you sooner. I suddenly remembered a bunch of stuff at once.
I see. So, you've been using my data to run your simulations.
Typical... Leave it to Tower to muddy the waters.
(What the...? She sounds so different all of a sudden!)
You're not a simulation... are you?
Uh-oh...
Princeton, remember the regulations...
Regulations? So THAT'S why you wouldn't say a word.
Well, I've already broken them, so it doesn't matter now...
Devil, I don't care whether you're real or a simulation. I'm taking you down.
Taking me down? Now?
...Say, do you have any idea how much of a mess you're in right now?
We're going to take you down... no matter what it takes.
Oh boy... If you think getting a game over here will bring you back to reality, I have bad news for you.
...What are you talking about? Once the simulation is over...
Do you know where you are right now?
Inside a simulation created by the Sea of Stars' systems?
And, do you know where that simulation space is located?
Actually, let's go on step further. Do you know how the "Galactic Core" is even able to generate simulated worlds in the first place?
I-I don't know. I just arrived at the Sea of Stars, so...
Really? First day on the job, and you got caught up in this mess. Ahahah!
How interesting. It really does make me feel better knowing there are folks out there with luck even worse than mine.
Since I'm in a good mood, I'll let you in on a little something. You see...
...Don't tell them? And who are you, telling me what not to do?
...Ah, of course. You've gotten good at concealing yourself.
...Fine, then I won't tell them. I'll just watch what happens.
...You'll really set me free if help you out?
...I already have a plan – to run off amidst all the chaos.
...That's a good idea. We have a deal.
Who are you talking to?
Nobody. My speech synthesizer was just on the fritz.
Listen up – I hate dealing with those Harbingers as much as you do.
So I'm going to help you out this one time.
Help you as much as this vessel is able to, anyway...
It's a cheap knockoff, but it's better than nothing.
I'll do something about those mist entities. You two run that-a-way in the meantime.
Yeah, toward that glowing thing. I'll send Laffey the exact coordinates.
Go through that and you'll be able to exit the simulation. Now go.
L-Laffey?
Yeah, the communications system sent me some coordinates. Glowy coordinates...
Seems we have no choice but to go and check it out.
You're off the hook this time, Devil. Let's go, Princeton.

Beacon.say: In the Sea of Stars control room, TB's battle with the hostile data raged on.

Beacon.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Main Terminal

Beacon.say: In crisis mode, I have full control of all resources within the Sea of Stars.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Beacon.say: In other words, there's no need for you to break the glass.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal

Beacon.say: Status report: I've successfully isolated the hostile data within a sandbox.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal

Beacon.say: At this time, it is impossible to delete the data.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal

Beacon.say: Due to the hostile data's attack on our communications system, I've taken the system offline to physically isolate it from said data.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal

Beacon.say: It will be impossible to leave the control room or communicate with the outside. I'm sorry.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Main Terminal

Beacon.say: Question: why have you mentioned the act of drawing?
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Beacon.say: You drew that on the screen. It's cute.
Beacon.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Main Terminal

Beacon.say: That is no drawing. It's simply a visual display of the affected area.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal

Beacon.say: But it looks like a drawing...
Beacon.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Main Terminal

Beacon.say: Performing visual data verification. No similar patterns found.
Beacon.factiontag: Main Terminal
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department
Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

sequence: This is Fortress. Correspondence attempt number 3122.
"I didn't expect them to run into Harbingers... What's the damage report?"
Three Raptor-class ships were moderately damaged. One Klyatva-class ship was lightly damaged.
The shields of flagship Calabrian Pride have been overloaded.
"The situation is still under control. Engage Ixion III to initiate repairs."
"You'll have to handle Calabrian Pride's shields yourself."
"Really, do they even know what a flagship is? The point isn't for it to rush in at the front."
I've detected a space-time distortion in the surrounding area.
"Will we be able to go home?!"
Warning: Distress signal received from a Council beacon.
Per protocol, we must go to their rescue immediately.
"No. We're going to SAVE our home!"

Guiding Darkness.say: Somewhere, in an unknown location...
The twilit sea was covered in a veritable layer of wreckage.
...We got away.
Princeton, where are we?
...Princeton?
Princeton... Where are you?
Nobody answered Laffey's calls. There were no signs of life on the sea, so calm that it looked like a mirror's surface.
Princeton... is gone...?
BEEP BEEP!
Laffey, can you hear me?
Guam...?
Phew... I saw that ray of light come down from the sky, but I didn't think it would be you...
Are Princeton and Northampton with you?
Northampton left the exercise early...
Princeton was with me until just a minute ago.
We were running from the black tornado, and then she was gone...
Running from a black tornado...? So were we!
Wait, you and Princeton got separated?!
Laffey, don't move a muscle! I'm on my way!
...When I went to the coordinates the Arbiter gave us, I was warped here.
It sounds almost supernatural...
*sob*... I thought this was a simulation, but then Devil suddenly started talking... It was so scary...
Argh... All this crazy stuff is happening, and I can't even broadcast it!
Princeton was going with Laffey to the exit, but she disappeared when Laffey warped here!
We can't reach her over comms. Maybe she was warped somewhere else!
Why do I feel like I've been dragged into a hot mess?!
Aaaaaaaaah!
San Jacinto, wh-where's the tornado?! It's not chasing us, is it?!
Phew... I don't see it around... I-it looks like we managed to get to safety.
...Constellation?
...Huh?
Wh-what's going on? Louisville? Flasher? Laffey? Why are you all here...?
More importantly... Where are we?
Uh, so it's a long story...
Do I have to explain everything again...
If you don't mind, Laffey! Pretty please!
Laffey's group was in the NY City experiment zone, while Guam's was in the Coral Sea. Both were chased off by the black tornado...
There must have been a major malfunction at the Galactic Core.
Could it have happened while I was investigating the abnormal data...?
Everyone, I'm sorry.
It's all my fault. If I wasn't so anxious to make progress...
Everyone tried to stop me, too, especially poor Flasher...
If only I'd been more careful... *sniffle*...
*sob*... I-it's okay... As long as you're okay, Stella, everything's gonna be fine...
Yeah! Hear that, Stella? We're fine as long as you're fine!
...Why are you calling me that, too?
Just livening up the show as any good star should!
There's no use crying over spilled milk. What's done is done.
Besides, it's not like it's the worst-case scenario.
TB said there wasn't any danger when you analyzed the data, right? So there was no way of knowing this would happen when you used the Reality Lens to study it.
Unless you just never studied it, this would've happened sooner or later.
It sucks that so many of us got wrapped up in it, but that just means we can all help each other out of this mess!
Imagine if you were the only one connected. Now THAT would be the worst case.
...Th-thank you for trying to console me...
Not even! This is rational analysis. You mentioned the Commander before you connected, too.
What kind of situation did the Commander get into? What adventures awaited, and how did the Commander escape danger?
You gave an example, and I say we should learn from it. Right?
Either way, no feeling sorry for ourselves! You know the Sea of Stars the most of all of us, so we're relying on you!
But you wouldn't all be in danger if I hadn't done anything, so relying on me is just...
Fine, then. Constellation, take responsibility and get us all outta here!
Ahahaha... You know how to hit her where it hurts.
Heheh. Do I?
Cheer up, Stella... I know you can get us all home safe!
Right! You can do it, Constellation!
We should find Princeton, too... *yawn*...
O-of course... I don't have time to feel sorry for myself right now...!
For the sake of those who believe in me, I have to do everything in my power to take everyone home!
There you go! You're on fire!
And don't you worry – I'll record all of your efforts for you!
Things might not be great right now, but it's cool if I record, right?
Of course. Those recordings will be necessary later.
Record as much as you like. When we get back, I'll have the Analysis Department go through the footage. I'll see that it's returned to you once they're done.
Woohoo!
Sorry to put you on the spot, but what's the plan, Boss?
U-umm... First, let's... search our surroundings? Yes, let's do that.
There must be a reason we were brought to the same place from simulations made by different machines.
Let's look around and find some clues!

Retrieval Protocol.say: Stella, my salvager is picking up a strange reading!
I knew there had to be something that gathered us all here in one place.
The signal source is underwater... I'll go have a look-see!
A while later, Flasher resurfaced with a metallic object in hand.
I'm back, Stella!
I found this th-thing!
Wait, I thought your salvage gear was designed to retrieve data?
Are you saying it can do regular salvage, too?
Ordinarily, you'd use the salvager to scan an object's data and send it to the Sea of Stars, but there's no telling if the Sea of Stars is operating at normal capacity now...
And there's no analysis device here, so... I just b-brought this thing up with me...
You've got a point. Analyzing it ourselves is our only option in this case.
Stella, this object gives off readings pretty similar to the abnormal data we found in the Coral Sea simulation!
It does? Let me have a look...
Flasher handed the device to Constellation, who began to inspect it.
Hmm... Metallic exterior. Structurally divided into two parts.
The stand is ten centimeters and the main unit is thirty. It looks like some sort of transmitter.
Considering the material, it's quite lightweight... The panel is damaged, leaving its circuitry exposed. It's clearly broken.
Manufacturer information... No, the letters are too worn to read.
Have you figured anything out?
Only that it's horribly destroyed. So, nothing useful.
Judging by the stand, I believe it's a floating transmitter. Meaning, it's a small beacon.
A... beacon?
Yes, the kind that aids in navigation. It might be what's responsible for warping us all here.
The strangest thing about this place is that, unlike those other simulations, it doesn't exist amongst the data we have.
So how in the world did we end up here? The only thing I can think of is that the Sea of Stars and the Reality Lens are linked to that future the Commander got sent to, but...
Uhh, I'm not following you. What exactly are you talking about?
Why we got warped here. All I have now is speculation, though.
At any rate, we have a clue as to how we got here. The next thing is to figure out what this place is.
Jacinto, did your aerial reconnaissance find anything?
I'll cut to the chase. There is absolutely nothing in the sea or on the islands.
There are no fish in the water or animals on land. Nor are there any signs of vegetation, insects, or even fungal life.
Finally, the thing that looks like a mountain range in the distance is actually a huge heap of trash.
To put it in other words, this space is made up almost entirely of man-made stuff.
Oh, and there is a flying artificial island not far from here. Almost looks Siren-made, if you ask me.
Here's a photo.
Oh... I recognize this. That's one of the Sirens' floating fortresses.
What's that tucked away in the corner? Some kind of materials storehouse?
It seems the Sirens are carrying out a huge resource collection project in this sea. Or maybe they've taken to recycling.
Either way, I've never seen anything like this.
Oh! What if we've happened upon a secret supply base of theirs?
That gives me an idea! Hang on a sec...
Guam cupped her hands together, scooped up a handful of seawater, and drank it in one gulp.
Wh-what are you doing?!
Um, there's water right here if you're thirsty, Guam.
Drinking water from the sea could make you sick...
I wasn't sure if the simulation ran that deep, but it was worth it to test my theory.
Jacinto, you must've gotten a good view of this area with your planes. Are you sure this is a sea?
Well... Yeah? Why do you ask?
Because I couldn't taste the sea in the seawater. It just tasted like water.
Really? I need to see for myself...
...You're right. It's no different from regular tap water. I don't like what that implies.
Flasher, did you bring any equipment that can analyze water?
Y-yeah, I did!
Great. Run an analysis on this sample.
Stella, it's done...
Not even trace amounts of minerals, let alone any salt. Nothing more than two hydrogens and an oxygen.
In other words, this entire ocean is made of distilled water.
That's so weird. Did the Sirens, like, filter out all the minerals?
Then this would be purification on an unprecedented scale. Seawater consists of around 3% salt, so...
BEEP BEEP!
I'm getting weird readings on my IFF system...
Weird readings? Can you glean anything from them?
Nope... Only their identification signals.
At least they're within radar detection range.
The terrain here is rather messy, so maybe the signals are from a Siren fleet in hiding?
Broadcasting ID signals isn't something Sirens typically do.
Though I guess they could be doing it to lure us into a trap.
I'm getting another signal, but this time it's different. I can't identify it...
If I may ask, how come only you are able to receive these signals anyway?
Might be because her rigging isn't her original rigging, but rather built on simulation data.
If I were them and I was waiting in ambush, I wouldn't want to put my target on high alert by sending signals to them.
Whoever's broadcasting these is most likely trying to tell us that they know we're here.
...At any rate, we won't get anything done by standing around.
Laffey, send an identification signal back. We'll see how they respond and go from there.

The United Council.say: The shipgirls assumed a combat-ready formation and sailed onward through the vast, distilled sea.
Each time the unidentified signal changed in frequency, Laffey updated her own signal in response.
The two sides had formed a strange kind of tacit understanding, even though neither one knew the meaning behind the other's signal.
As they circled around an artificial island, they finally laid eyes on the source of the signal.
Is that fleet of warships... floating in the air?
I'm not seeing things, am I?
You're not. Their hulls are definitely not touching the water.
If I had to guess, they're floating at least ten meters above the surface.
There are so many of them... but they don't show up on the radar.
Whoa, whoa, are they rocking the same stealth tech as the Ashes?!
Pardon my potty mouth, but holy shit...
Ashes camouflaging themselves is one thing, but these are hundred-meter-long ships!
Regardless of how unbelievable the shipgirls thought it, the very real fleet made up of dozens of ships was slowly approaching them.
They have several corvettes, and what looks like battleships and aircraft carriers.
The biggest one has gotta be their flagship.
Probably, yeah. It's over a kilometer long... It's more like a fortress than a ship.
And to think we call our simulation opponents "the fleet of the future."
This fleet makes those things look like toys...
What in the world ARE these guys?
You tell me. Some science fiction starfleet, I guess?
BEEP BEEP!
...They're sending us an unencrypted broadcast.
This is the Council's Task Force 5, addressing the KAN-SEN in our path.
Your identification signal cannot be parsed.
State your fleet number and objective in this area at once.
Such a monotone voice... It strongly reminds me of TB's. Do you think they have an AI, too?
TB's voice is a lot more friendly, if you ask me.
Constellation, you should probably give them a swift answer.
R-right. Quick communication is essential in cases like this...
Err... Actually, you handle it, Guam! Find out who they are and what they want!
You got it! This pretty presenter has you covered!
Ahem. This is the Eagle Union's... uh... Sea of Stars Testing Fleet!
Now I have a question for you! What is "the Council" and how did you get here?
Querying database for "Eagle Union Sea of Stars Testing Fleet"...
No results matching those keywords found.
I'll take that to mean you are a classified Union-affiliated fleet. Is that correct?
It's not wrong, considering how hush-hush the Sea of Stars is... So, yeah, we're a classified fleet!
Activating scanning module...
Abnormal readings detected.
Warning: Category V corrosion threat detected. Alert mode has been engaged as per security protocols.
What is a "Category V corrosion threat"?
*sniffle*... W-warning? That doesn't sound good...
Order: explain why the Council beacon in your possession has been damaged.
Order: provide the current location of the fleet to which the beacon originally belonged.
Order: lay down your arms and submit to an inspection.
Hey! What's this all of a sudden?!
We found the beacon just lying there underwater! We have no idea who it belonged to!
Also, "lay down our arms?" Fat chance! Who do you think you are to boss us around?
Repeat: this is the Council's Task Force 5.
Order: lay down your arms and submit to an inspection.
Thanks for the non-answer! What the hell is this "Council" anyway?!
Diagnosis: KAN-SEN group has been exposed to Category V corrosion.
Corrosion Stage: Initial.
Symptoms: Hallucinations, cognitive dissociation.
Corrosion Source: Harbingers.
Recommended Actions: Neutralize targets, non-lethally if possible. Following neutralization, administer sedative agents and cure corrosion.
Initiating anti-corrosion measures now.
It sounds like they think we're infected with something. Poor you, Guam.
Is that how you see me? As some hallucinating whackjob?
Now I'm angry! Whether you're an AI or not, you've obviously got junk for brains!
You're not even a hundredth, no, a thousandth as good as TB! I'm gonna send you to the scrapheap!
Umm, seeing as she said they'd go easy on us, maybe we shouldn't retaliate with full force?
Hmph. No, we're giving them all we've got for the sake of the show! Isn't that right, Laffey?
Both ways are fine by me.
(I have many questions, but it seems I'll have to sit on them until the fighting is over.)
(At least we'll gather some data while we're at it. You don't do battle with flying warships every day.)
Since they're holding back, we should give them the same courtesy! Prepare for battle!

The Hoverfleet.say: Oof... They neutralized my electronic warfare systems...
Look out for the shockwaves of their bombs!
Their aircraft are suppressing us. They've got the clear upper hand in the air!
Are those aircraft they're launching actually drones or what?
They're unmanned, that much I can tell for sure.
The ships themselves, those are nothing like the mass-produced ships we know.
And they don't even touch the water, they hover above it... *sob*...
There, there, it's okay. It's not your fault.
(Is that why they fly? As a countermeasure against submarines? But surely it takes an inordinate amount of energy to keep them airborne...)
(Maybe the energy cost isn't a big issue to them. Like if their energy source is practically limitless...)
We're struggling here, Constellation. Should we retreat?
I'll stay here and try to hold out a little longer.
They don't want to sink us, and we need to take advantage of that.
Keep fighting! Get as much data as possible to bring back home!

Protocol Warfare.say: KABOOOM!
Ugh... They are trying HARD to disarm us! Half of my cannons are inoperable!
Stella, my reliable and sweet genius, can we please retreat already?!
As much as I'd like to gather more data... you're right. We'll be in danger if we stay any longer.
Jacinto made an escape route for us. Let's retreat to the Siren base I just marked on the map.
If there are any Sirens there, they're more likely to target the flying fleet than us! Good thinking!
And while they're duking it out, that's when we get out of Dodge!
Just then, a massive machine flew forth, leaving the floating fleet's formation.
There was an error in the data. These girls have not been corroded.
All ships, cease fire. Flagship's orders.
Uh-oh... That rigging...
I am War Protocol Fortress, member of the Council's Task Force 5.
She's a Siren!
What?
Oh god, oh man! What now, Constellation?!
Pitting Sirens against other Sirens isn't gonna work! We need a new plan, stat!
I know! I couldn't possibly have predicted this!
Running away isn't going to work... It's highly likely the Siren is in control of those ships.
But not all hope is lost yet.
That one looks like their control unit, so if we just defeat her, the rest of the fleet should be easy pickings!
Hold on a–
KABOOOM!
What?

The Hoverfleet's Strategy.say: The shipgirls were locked in a fierce battle, only barely holding up the frontline against the swarms of drones.
Constellation, look! Their damaged drones are all landing on that saucer-shaped thing!
Yeah. That must be their repair machine. One more thing I noticed, Guam...
They're being extremely conservative with their warships.
You have a point, though. We haven't actually disabled any of their warships, despite hitting them with all we've got.
They retreat back as soon as they get slightly scratched up. Hell, some of the Sirens use themselves as meatshields to protect their warships.
Exactly. For whatever reason, they're going out of their way not to take any losses.
I think we can turn this around if we're able to exploit that!
In other words, focus on one target at a time?
Correct! Everyone, I'll designate an enemy – focus fire on it!

Convergence.say: Targets are acting with hostility. Initiating combat mode.
Eliminating threats now.
KABOOOM!
Stop, Fortress! These girls are not a threat to us!
Did Princeton just... come down from the sky?
Isn't she... META?
Why is a META siding with the Sirens? What's the meaning of this?
Everyone, cease fire and step back for now!
We lose contact for just a while, and look what happens...
I TOLD you to stop attacking, Fortress. What's the matter with you?
...They attacked me first.
Don't make excuses.
Ms. Silver Fox has summoned you. She probably wants you to fix our comms system so things like this won't happen again!
...Fine. Deal with these girls for me, then.
*sigh*... Hey, you KAN-SEN!
Who? Us?
Who do you think? Yes, you.
Are you really with the Eagle Union? Why did you attack one of the Council's fleets?
And after I expressly asked Ms. Silver Fox to double-check that you don't have any corrosion on you...
I can't cover for you if you attack friends and foes indiscriminately, you know.
Which of you is in charge? Who gave the order?
Princeton is fuming...
Guam...
(Uh-oh, she's got that "Guam, you better say something" look!)
Ahem. I am Eagle Union large cruiser Guam, hull number CB-2. I'm in charge of this fleet.
*sigh*... No, you aren't, Guam.
Constellation is.
...How did you know?
It's obvious. No sane person would put her in charge of your fleet.
Excuse me? Do you even know who I am?
I do – all too well, even. I'm from the Union myself, in case you forgot.
I know all of you. You, Constellation, Laffey, San Jacinto, Louisville, Flasher...
You know who I am, too. You called me Princeton just a minute ago.
And yet, you treat me like a stranger.
You don't recognize the Council or Fortress, and I can't parse your identification signals...
So Ms. Silver Fox was right. You ARE from another time and space.
Great. Seems we're going to be here for a while.
Fortress, set up a temporary base.
Upon receiving Princeton's orders, the floating warships came to life.
Starting with the capital ships in the rear of the fleet, the steel giants slowly began descending to the surface.
After settling in the water, the decks of the warships moved aside, exposing the cannons inside their hulls.
Looking at the bores of these cannons stirred a sense of security in one's soul.
Following their larger brethren, the massive repair ship and the vessels damaged in the battle finally touched down and began repairs.
You're really hunkering down, I see.
I was wondering what weapons those ships in the back had, since they didn't fight in the battle. Turns out they have large-caliber guns stowed away inside.
They need to be in the water in order to use them, don't they?
Yup. Those are citadel ships, they're our main defense when we set up a temporary base.
Now that we have time to talk, let me explain the situation from our perspective.
Before arriving here, we got into a space-time anomaly. It appeared without warning, damaging a lot of our equipment to the point of rendering it nonfunctional.
As a result, we jumped the gun and attacked you before we knew whether you were corroded or not.
For that, I apologize on behalf of all of us.
We are not your enemy and we mean no harm to you.
I hope that clears the air between us. I'm sure we both have many questions, so let's talk inside.
Oh, and Guam, no photography is allowed inside the restricted areas. Understood?
Ahahaha... Fine, fine!

The Hidden Crown.say: Task Force 5 - Temporary Base
Alright, so, there are a few differences between you and the versions of you that I'm familiar with.
It's not easy to describe the differences, but if I had to say, it's like you're from a parallel universe.
You should know what I mean if you've read any old science fiction.
The most striking difference from our perspective is that you have flying warships.
Well, science and technology develop quickly when the need arises.
Is this your world, by the way? How do you live here?
We don't! The branch we come from isn't nearly this ruined.
It can't be your world either, seeing as you asked that question.
Nope. The world we come from is... um, pretty far off.
We were just sailing in our home branch until we picked up a distress signal coming from a Council beacon.
It wasn't that far away, so we took it upon ourselves to come to the rescue.
We followed the signal for a while, and then all of a sudden we got caught in the space-time anomaly I mentioned before.
Then this and that happened and we wound up here.
Now, how did you get your hands on that Council beacon?
By "beacon," you mean this device... right?
It's a long story... Sorry, I can't think straight now. I've been up late lately and haven't gotten much sleep...
I think I'm going to pass out... Guam, take it from here...
Huh? Wait, I can't just– Oh, she's out cold.
Heehee. Knowing her, she's been staying up to gaze at the stars.
It's a strange feeling, knowing a person so well even though you've never met them before.
You heard her, Guam. Kindly explain it to me.
Okay. Well, it started this morning when I was in the hangar...
To be frank, I don't think I understood 10% of that.
Branches can be as different as day and night, it seems...
I know one thing for sure, though – you found the beacon simply by chance.
(Just when I thought we could go home. Of course it wouldn't be that easy...)
Yup. We got wrapped up in this, that's all. Now, think you could answer some of our questions?
Of course.
Did you see Princeton on your way here?
Err, by that I mean another version of you.
Nope. You said you got separated from that Princeton before you found the beacon, no?
So if I had to guess, she's somewhere in this ocean. Her comms equipment might be malfunctioning, like ours.
We'll help you find her.
Fortress, start a search for Princeton.
Orders received.
Thanks a bunch! Uh...
*staaaaare*...
Laffey has been glaring at Fortress for some time now.
Not only that, she and the rest of you attacked her for no reason when you saw her.
Why? Did she do something wrong in your home branch?
"Something" is putting it mildly.
She's a Siren. How can you fight side by side with her like this?
There seems to be a misunderstanding here. Fortress is an ANTI-X who has been specially modified to serve the Council.
And I am a KAN-SEN who also serves the Council. Why would we NOT fight together?
For that matter, what is a "Siren?"
ANTI-X...? Oh, right, the Commander's files said that's one of the names they go by.
In our branch, the Sirens are our enemy!
Wait, you're saying that ANTI-X and your "Sirens" are the same? And they're your enemies?
I don't understand. Why would the ANTI-X and KAN-SEN be at war? What is there to fight over...?
What do you mean "why?" They came out of nowhere and wreaked havoc on our seas!
They tried to take everything from us! As shipgirls, it's our duty to get rid of them and reclaim what belongs to humanity!
The Sirens are our enemies and must be destroyed at any cost!
...How could that be?
What about X, then? What happened to your war with them?
The whole reason we thought you had been corroded was because our AI detected the presence of Harbingers.
Don't tell me you're waging wars on both X AND the ANTI-X at the same time?
Huh? The Sirens are our number one enemy. You keep saying "X," and I don't even know what that is.
Princeton, you have a message from the flagship. Ms. Silver Fox is calling for you.
Okay, I'll be right there. Watch these girls while I... Actually, that's not such a good idea.
*staaaaare*...
*yaaawn*... Sorry for dozing off like that.
We'll be fine here. Plus, I want to get a closer look at Fortress.
Alright. Fortress, keep them company.
Wow, you weren't out for long, Constellation. I thought you'd sleep all day.
I would have, but then my stomach started growling.
Which is strange, because you shouldn't be able to feel hunger or thirst in a simulation, and yet...
"Simulation?" What?
Oh, nothing.
...Fortress, get started on an early dinner. I'll eat with you when I get back.
Orders received.
I'd better get going now.
Sorry, one last thing! If you have any books that detail the history of your branch, could I borrow them?
We have some time to kill before dinner, after all.
Hmm... Yes, the information you want should be in our supply vessel's library.
Give me a sec, I'll copy the files over for...
Huh? Access denied? Fortress, what's going on?
You should go to the flagship first. It's possible Ms. Silver Fox is getting impatient and has limited your access.
It wouldn't surprise me if she did.
I'll be back in a bit. If anything happens, we'll discuss it later.
*sigh*...
Hang in there, Yawny McYawnface. Dinner's just around the corner.
That wasn't a yawn, it was... Ah, forget it.
You wouldn't understand.
"Wouldn't understand"? I think I know the situation pretty well, considering I was the one asking the questions!
Fine. I was just thinking about something...
Why would this "Silver Fox" restrict Princeton's access?
Iunno. To make her hurry up?
I don't think it's as simple as that. Princeton trusts us, whereas Silver Fox clearly doesn't.
Getting important information out of her is not going to be easy.

Silver Fox.say: An hour later, Princeton META returned from her visit to the flagship.
Sorry, that took longer than I expected.
Was your access issue resolved?
Uh, more or less. As embarrassing as it is to admit, it happened because of another malfunction.
Now, it's pretty late, so shall we have dinner?
Princeton stood up and suddenly noticed that on the low table behind her, there was a small heap of snacks piled up.
Is... Is that Oxy-cola Classic?!
And tortillas, and chips, and fruits, too!
Are these all yours?
Yup. We heard all your food is rations that put shelf life over taste, so we thought we'd bring out some food we took with us.
...You use precious storage space on treats and snacks when you're going into battle?
(We weren't exactly expecting to go into battle today, so...)
Umm, these are things we just grabbed on the fly. Aren't your standard-issue naval MREs a lot more satisfying than this stuff?
Wait... What is that stuff? You guys ARE a formal fighting force, right?
*sigh*... We have to settle for synthetic food when we're on active duty.
To tell the truth, I don't know how many years since I last had real snacks or fruit...
Princeton looked at the cans of cola with a longing twinkle in her eyes.
Well, now you can have as much as you like. Don't be shy.
There is more than enough to go around.
Really? Oh, man! Don't mind if I do!
Phew! That hits the spot! I've missed this taste!
I've tried again and again to replicate it ever since they changed the recipe, but I never got it right.
They changed Oxy-cola's recipe?
Yeah... If only the recipe hadn't been lost in the war...
The war...?
Err! I misspoke! There was no war!
You're not allowed to speak about it, are you?
...Yeah. No use in trying to hide it from you.
See, we have this brilliant scientist on board.
She told me you have to be very cautious when dealing with a space-time anomaly.
Anything I do could have a butterfly effect, so the range of things I can discuss with you is extremely limited.
Is this scientist "Ms. Silver Fox," by any chance?
Well, it looks like the lady herself wants to greet you. Fortress, reroute the comms lines to me.
Understood. Rerouting now.
Rad vstreche, travelers. I am Silver Fox, a member of the Council.
Is that a codename? Are you silver like the color of your hair, and a fox because you're as cunning as one?
I didn't choose this codename, so I can't say if the fox part is accurate or not.
However, yes, the silver part is because of my hair color.
Ah. If I may ask, would it be possible for us to speak to you in person?
Unfortunately, no. The world I inhabit – the world I have seen – is not a good place for you to be.
For a myriad of reasons, it's best that we don't meet face to face.
Furthermore, we should refrain from learning too much about each other in order to minimize the impact on the space-time continuum.
I see... Sorry for the sudden question, but do you believe in fate?
Do I believe in an omnipresent entity that has determined a course for everyone?
No, I do not.
Okay, then what do you think about miracles that occur by happenstance?
Philosophy is not my specialty. Does this tie into your previous question about fate?
I do horoscope reading as a hobby. Some people dismiss it as just confirmation bias, of course...
But I divined multiple times today, and the result was the same each time: "things will go well for you."
You can chalk that up to random chance, sure, but I believe in miracles even so.
You're describing a self-fulfilling prophecy. You believe in fate, so you only give weight to things that go in your favor.
Let's not forget that you ran into trouble before you arrived here, and also got separated from a friend.
Do you really consider those among the things that "went well" for you?
I mean...
Furthermore, even the things you think are positive may not be so in reality.
The beacon you found was not only physically damaged, but also the data within it has been corroded by X.
While we are repairing and purifying it, it may turn out to be beyond salvaging.
Now, let's finally consider the possibility that our meeting was no work of fate, no miracle, no coincidence.
You were drawn to the beacon because your equipment pointed you to it. So did ours. We simply headed toward the same point independently.
...I suppose you're right.
I can only hope and pray your repairs go well. That beacon may be our key to getting home.
Getting home... Yes, perhaps.
As you said, it's probably for the best if we don't ask each other big questions.
But, I'd hate to completely pass on this golden opportunity. Can't we just ask a few questions and no more than that?
Only if they're benign. I do not want to take any risks here.
Gosh, you're quite insistent...
That I am. People often say I'm a risk-averse person.
Seeing as serious questions are off the table, why don't we have some fun with the format?
What do you mean?
By turning it into a quiz!
It's simple – we'll start by asking a few questions. If you can't answer them, that's totally fine.
Then when our turn is over, YOU get to ask US questions.
And the amount of questions you can ask is the same as the amount of our questions you answered, and on top of that, we'll answer ANY question no matter what!
This way, the initiative is all yours. You can control how much or how little information we exchange.
How's that? Sounds like a good arrangement, doesn't it?
Hmm...
You really thought this through. Alright, I'll bite.
Let's say you can ask up to six questions. One for each of you, including your Princeton.
Gotcha!
(Nice quick thinking, Guam!)
(Heh. Good improv skills are a must in my line of work!)
...It seems we'll have to postpone that quiz for a while.
A corrosion phenomenon – or "black tornado," as you call it – has appeared.
Oh, dear. Do you think the beacon attracted it, like how it attracted us?
We're dealing with a stubborn storm, eh...
Fortress, defend the base. I'll take the fleet and deal with it.
Let us at 'em, too!
Ms. Silver Fox, do we let them?
Yes. Go ahead.
Okay. Everyone, prepare for battle and follow me!

Corrosion Phenomenon.say: The shipgirls left the base and looked up at the sky to see a handful of warships had already taken flight.
At a glance, I think that's only about a tenth of your total fleet...
Are you sure that'll be enough?
It's all we need to deal with a phenomenon of this size.
So, what are we supposed to do?
We want to help, but I don't think us shooting into a storm is going to make it go away.
Shooting it does work, it's just not the most efficient. Sadly, we're in short supply of the weapons most effective against it, so we'll take the simplest approach.
Let me brief you on what a corrosion phenomenon is. It's abnormal weather that manifests only when multiple corrosion-capable entities gather, or when particular singular entities appear.
These phenomena largely dissipate on their own after a while and can only exist for prolonged periods in specific environments.
X's corrosion hasn't set in deeply in this area, and the environment isn't suited for sustained outbreaks either.
That is to say, this storm is actually very weak and will disappear by itself if left alone.
So what you're saying is, we don't really have to do anything.
In practice, though, we still need to deal with it. Otherwise it will corrupt the things around it and eventually lay waste to this whole area.
Since we lack the resources to attack the tornado directly, we'll have to eliminate the corrosion entities that spawn near it.
The more we eliminate, the faster the phenomenon will die down.
You don't want to sleep to the sound of a storm raging outside, do you?
Heck no! Let's deal with it so we can all sleep soundly!

Incompatibility.say: Princeton, unidentified forces are approaching our location.
Unidentified? They must be from that floating island base!
It's been dormant this whole time, so why are they showing themselves now?
Based on our aerial reconnaissance, the force was just manufactured at the base.
Sending images now.
They look like low-level ANTI-X combat units, but I've never seen models like these before...
Do you recognize them, Fortress?
I do not.
I do!
I saw 'em in Iron Blood documents the Commander shared with us. These are Enforcers belonging to Arbiter: The Lovers VI.
This first variant is called Combination. It has drones that resemble sea angels, specializing in support and jamming.
The other variant is named Attraction. That one is all about firepower.
Enforcers...? I've never heard of those before.
(I have read about the Lovers in an ANTI-X plan proposal before...)
(But that's all it should be – a proposal.)
Fortress, can you communicate with those units?
I tried, but I cannot reach them or transfer control of them.
The protocols they use must differ from mine.
In addition, they shot down my monitoring drone. For this reason, I believe they are hostile.
Wait, they shot it down? But they're your fellow ANTI-X... It must have been some kind of mistake.
...Huh? A call from Ms. Silver Fox?
...Okay. Got it.
What did she say?
Those "Sirens" are not our allies.
She said, "stand back and let them and the corrosion entities fight each other, then finish off what remains."
Hang on, did she refer to them as "Sirens"?
That she did. Probably so it would be easier for you to follow.
Also, she said that we're allowed to fight back if they attack us.
...I admire her swift decision-making skills.
Sorry, I'm getting sidetracked. Let's change positions and watch the battle from afar.

Wrong Destination.say: The battle between the Sirens and the corrosion entities unfolded as predicted.
The shipgirls had already left the combat area, monitoring for developments with their aircraft from relative safety.
The storm died down, and the Enforcers and their warships had suffered serious casualties.
It was the perfect opportunity for the girls to swoop in and quickly crush what remained of the enemy.
Time elapsed since the storm manifested: 1 hour, 20 minutes.
After the fighting was over...
Hmm... This doesn't feel right.
I don't expect a storm to leave a wreckage, but why didn't the Sirens when they're so similar to the ANTI-X?
There's practically nothing left to salvage even after all that fighting.
I hope that floating island has something worth our while.
Let us gather supplies while we're there.
Of course. It's time to fill our warehouse to the brim.
I've never heard of someone stealing supplies from the Sirens before.
Is it common practice to procure items locally in your branch?
Yeah. Don't want useful resources to go to waste. You can extend your fleet's operational lifespan by several years if you get lucky.
Sheesh... Fortress rivals an Arbiter in strength when she gets serious.
In retrospect, I'm really grateful you went easy on us earlier.
Yeah, she means business. The whole Task Force does.
And so does every other one. Our Task Forces are the best of the best in our world.
If we didn't pack this much heat, we wouldn't still be around.
Having said that, I think even your world's fleets could handle these guys without too much difficulty.
Anyway... Fortress, are you certain those enemies employ the same technology as the ANTI-X?
Yes. These so-called Enforcers have designs, weaponry, and tactics markedly similar to the ANTI-X.
Based on the presence of modules more advanced than mine, I theorize that the units above them – "Arbiters" – are newer models than I.
However, I should state that these Enforcers do not appear to be frontline units.
Their firepower configuration suggests that they are most likely logistics or facility defense units.
So we basically wiped out a support fleet?
Sounds like it. Considering the state of this place, that floating artificial island is likely their supply base.
It would explain why they weren't so tough.
You say that, but it's always a headache when WE have to deal with Enforcers.
Fair enough. Also, there's one thing I've been wondering about...
Is something wrong with your weapons? They're not as powerful as I expected them to be.
I didn't notice it during your battle with Fortress, since you were both holding back. This time you weren't, though, and I couldn't help but notice.
*staaaaare*...
Hey, I'm not saying you're weak! Just that I thought you'd be stronger.
Maybe your hulls have taken damage? I could see how that would lead to a drop in power.
Would you mind if Fortress scanned you real quick?
Our hulls might've been damaged...?
Scan us! Please!
Great. Fortress, do your thing.
Orders received. Commencing scan.
Sometime later...
Nope, they're in perfect condition... That leaves only one other explanation.
There may be something limiting your Wisdom Cubes.
You can't modify a Cube like that after it has formed a hull, so it had to be done before then...
Hmm. Yeah, I think someone put a limiter on your Cubes before your hulls were created.
Huh? Someone tampered with our Cubes?
Yes. The signs are there, even if I don't know how exactly they did it.
One reason why they might have put a limiter in place is that it improves the Cube's stability.
That has the benefit of slowing down METAmorphosis to a crawl as long as your hull doesn't get damaged and you don't suffer external corrosion.
It sounds less like a limiter and more like a safety feature.
Because, you know, isn't not turning META a good thing?
Well, generally, it is.
Limiting your combat ability just to avoid METAmorphosis is putting the cart before the horse, though.
How are shipgirls supposed to fight X when they don't have the strength to do it?
It's pointless to have a limiter if the enemy just sinks you before you even turn META.
I can understand wanting more power, but I'd think twice if it costs my sanity.
That's reasonable. I've seen what METAmorphosis does to people.
Nobody wants to turn out like them. Not me, either.
Obviously, we have reservations about letting corrosion seep into our hulls, but there is a way to manage it.
You're saying there's a way to reverse the corrosion?
No, but I know the next best thing.
See, I have a device that allows me to stop my hull's corrosion at the surface level.
Then after I'm done fighting, I purge that corrosion and my hull returns to its pre-transformation state.
Oh, oh! It's like a superhero costume you put on and take off!
Uhh... I don't quite get the analogy, but I'll assume it's close enough.
The takeaway is this: X's corrosion isn't natural, but your hull changing is. You don't need to be deathly afraid of it.
You still need to be wary of not going past the critical point, because that's when you risk becoming mentally unstable.
If that happens, all you can do is hope your friends will give you intensive medical care and try to do everything possible to save you.
Sounds frightening...
It is. It's probably why they put limiters on your Cubes in the first place – to make absolutely sure it doesn't happen.
In fact, they went even further with your Type II rigging, Laffey. It effectively erases the corrosion pathway, meaning you can't undergo METAmorphosis even if you wanted to.
(I'm more surprised that the Type II rigging has already been completed. The project was only at its infancy when...)
Good to know.
Is there any way to remove the limiters on our Cubes?
Whoa, whoa, you're not seriously considering it, are you?
I'm not. I'm just asking out of curiosity.
I honestly don't know. Modifying an existing hull takes an immense amount of work.
Fortress would have to run some analyses to find you an answer.
My system resources are occupied by other tasks at the moment. Shall I reallocate them?
No. Focus on your current tasks. Analyzing the beacon, searching for your missing person, recovering supplies, repairing the fleet...
Sorry, but we've got our hands tied, Constellation. Ask again later.
Okay...
Ah, right. I forgot to mention one important thing.
Ms. Silver Fox wants to give you all the Council's Unity Medal!
A medal? For what?
For helping us during this operation! We are truly grateful for it.
I mean, we didn't really do much. Hell, we did more harm than good to your warships in particular.
Do we really deserve a medal for that?
You also recovered one of the Council's beacons and assisted us in combat. Come on, you've earned it.
By the way, would you like a physical medal or an e-medal?
An... e-medal?
Yes? Oh, I guess you don't have those yet. Physical it is, then.
I like the physical ones better, too.
Let me just tell the 3D printer to get on that... There. We'll hand it to you at the base once it's done.
You can go back early if you'd like. I'll take the fleet and patrol the area for a while.
Besides, you still have your "quiz" to do, don't you?

Six Questions.say: After the battle, the gang returned to the temporary base.
As promised, Silver Fox did not show up in person. Instead, Fortress was used to relay messages back and forth between them.
On behalf of the Council, I would like to thank you all for your valiant fighting during today's battle.
We've done nothing that you should thank us for.
All we did was help the flying fleet destroy the corrosion entities. We hardly played a major role.
Don't be so modest. Big or small, we value all assistance.
I learned a proverb when I visited the Dragon Empery: "Every grain of dirt makes a mountain, and every drop of rain erodes stone."
The Council's Unity Medal was made to appreciate such efforts.
Now, regarding that quiz you mentioned. Have you already thought of questions you'd like to ask?
Yes. We're all ready.
Good. Heehee, I wonder what kind of exciting questions you'll ask me?
Go on, Constellation. You may begin.
Okay, Ms... Silver Fox, I'd like to start off strong.
Why does the Council's fleet float above the water?
Making a fleet hover above rather than ride on the water must consume considerable energy.
Yes, you're right. Making ships levitate does take a toll on one's energy stores.
However, our fleet is powered by Energy Cubes, so a shortage of power is the least of our concerns.
As for why we actively choose to make them levitate, that's simply because travel ON the water is dangerous.
In practice, traveling on the water leads to far greater losses than taking the extra effort to fly.
Is the reason for that related to the X...?
(No freebie questions, I guess...)
Ahem. Okay, second question.
What exactly is the Council, as an organization?
I thought that would be your first question.
And I think I'd be glad to answer that for you.
It's a cross-faction organization formed to pool all of the resources of humanity toward fighting the X. Its full name is the United Council.
A real political entity with real power that crosses factional borders. You could liken it to the Global Consortium, but stronger.
Its authority and resources far outstrip any other international entity. Likewise does its military, of course.
...Global Consortium? International entities...? Is it similar to the Azur Lane, then?
Would that be your third question?
Oh, no... Sorry, I was asking offhandedly. That isn't my third question.
I'll take that to mean that I don't need to answer it, then. Next question, please.
(Asking offhandedly in the course of a conversation doesn't work, either. She won't let me through that easily...)
(Okay. I'll try asking something more pointed this time.)
I'm ready to ask my third question now.
The term "X" has been thrown around a lot. What does it mean?
At a press conference, one might call them... a particular hostile lifeform.
But I imagine that answer doesn't satisfy you. As we're providing each other with information, allow me to give you a kinder answer.
If the name X does not mean anything to you, then presumably, such an entity has not yet been observed in your branch.
As such, it would be best if you didn't know much about them yet. Knowing too much can be dangerous, and that isn't a threat.
My official answer is... I can't comment on that.
What about the Antiochus, or ANTI-X, then?
We know that they are weapons built to fight against X, but what exactly are they?
This is officially my fourth question
Hmm...
You're not going to brush this one off too, are you?
No; telling you should be fine in itself. But the ANTI-X is a plan of such large scale that it's difficult to answer in few words.
Hmm... "Artificial Navy Transformational Intelligence, Class X," or ANTI-X for short, is a weapon devised to deal with unknown threats.
The guiding principle behind it is to maintain combat capabilities for as long as possible – regardless of casualties or environment.
As an autonomous weapon, the humanoid and mass-produced ship units are powered by Energy Cube arrays.
There also exist multiple command units, each controlled by artificial intelligence with separate artificial personalities in order to support the division of labor.
...That product of an arms race against a totally unknown enemy ended up finding use in fighting against the X.
Heh. I don't know whether to call it lucky or unlucky...
Will that answer suffice?
Yes. You've offered more information than expected. Thank you.
Then your next question, please.
(The floating fleet, the Council, the Antiochus... So far, Ms. Silver Fox has answered three of my four questions.)
(That must mean that she has as many questions for us.)
(I have two more questions. Let's get to the hard ones...)
Thank you for answering my questions so far. On to the fifth.
Why have you come here?
No comment.
I see what you're doing, asking uncomfortable questions that I can't answer. You don't want me to get all six of my questions, do you?
You've got a good head on your shoulders. Just what I'd expect from Lexington's little sister.
(...Hm? Why did she bring up Lexington?)
(That's it. They know about Type II rigging. Maybe their Lexington...)
(No, no... That question alone wouldn't possibly yield the information I need.)
(Besides, whether Lexington has Type II rigging in their branch or not...)
(It would be meaningless if she just answered yes to that question. And what would I do if she said no?)
(Would I tell them about my Lexington and ask for help? But then...)
(Calm down, Constellation... This is all part of her scheme. Just ask the questions we all agreed on!)
This is your last question, so think carefully about it, little girl.
(She's answered three questions so far. We at least have minimal results.)
(It's obvious that she will answer some questions, but I doubt she will if I ask something even more pointed...)
(As planned, I'll ask her the thing I'm the most curious about...)
Who are you?
That is our final question.
Answer: I am the Council fleet's AI, War Protocol Fortress.
...Huh?
Why did you answer?!
Heehee. I promise we're not lying or brushing off your question.
You've been asking Fortress all of these questions, so of course, Fortress answered.
Tch... Must you be so difficult at the very end?
The floating fleet, the Council, the ANTI-X, and Fortress. We've answered four of your questions.
That means you will have to answer four of mine. Such are the rules, no?
Yes...
Then it's my turn now. Are you ready, Lexington's little sister?

Six Questions - Part Two.say: The two-sided quiz between the shipgirls and Silver Fox entered its second phase.
As per the rules Guam came up with, it was now Constellation's turn to answer Silver Fox's questions.
...Okay, let us hear your questions.
Certainly. My first question is this...
Do you know what the moon's soil consists of?
Huh? No, I have no idea.
I really hope that one day humanity will explore the stars.
But, even if we had the technology to do it today, who knows when we'll actually make our first voyage into space.
Almost all our resources go toward fighting the Sirens, so...
Didn't you say that you're from a base called the "Sea of Stars," though?
That's an... odd naming choice for a civilization that has yet to travel to the stars.
Second question.
What kind of place is the Sea of Stars?
I don't have the clearance to tell you that. Everything in the Sea of Stars is top secret.
...In hindsight, I shouldn't even have disclosed its name to you.
Wait, really?
Yes. We are not supposed to even mention it to outsiders.
Should I interpret your answer as, "no comment"?
I'm afraid so. I really cannot answer it.
Don't worry about it. Third question.
Where did your Wisdom Cubes come from?
I can't answer that.
Wisdom Cubes are a scientific miracle, developed with all of humanity's scientific knowledge sometime after the Azur Lane was established.
In our war against the Sirens, they also were the tipping point for things to swing in humanity's favor.
We KAN-SEN are born from Cubes, and we use their power to fight the Sirens.
I believe our world's governments possess the knowledge of how to make Cubes, but their production requires an exceedingly rare mineral.
As a result, only a small limited number of them have been made.
That is everything I know.
They require a rare mineral and not many have been produced... Interesting.
Very well. Next question.
How would you describe the Sirens' organizational structure?
If that's hard to answer, you can just describe what kinds of Sirens you've fought.
It's difficult to summarize. Do you mind if Guam answers for me?
Not at all. Go ahead.
Roger-dodger! Guam is here live to bring you the truth and the facts about the Sirens!
Leave your eccentricity at the door, please.
Ugh. Fiiine.
Ahem. The Sirens' most basic units are different kinds of warships, imitations of other shipgirls, called Pawns, and humanoid low-level combat units.
Above those, there are the elite Sirens who possess self-awareness.
Examples include Purifier, Tester, Omitter, Compiler, and Observer, among others.
They each have different fighting styles and equipment.
Oh, and we've already destroyed Compiler's mainframe, so she's out of the picture!
Above even the elite Sirens there are the elusive Arbiters and the Enforcers they control.
You should've gotten a gauge on the Enforcers' strength in the battle earlier.
Arbiters are a tough fight even when they don't occupy their vessels, but when they do, they pose a serious challenge even versus an entire fleet.
They inflict heavy casualties even if you do beat them. And if you don't... well, you can probably guess.
Heehee. I see. Moving on to the next question.
Have you heard of Project Dawn?
Umm... Project Dawn?
I'll answer this one, Guam.
(She's asked four questions about the moon's soil, the Sea of Stars, Wisdom Cubes, and the Sirens. We've answered three of them.)
Should I interpret your answer as, "no comment"?
If you do not answer it, you will have no choice but to answer the next one.
If you're trying to fool me to get around the question, I fear that it won't work so well in your case.
You're a kind, honest girl, and deceit is not your strong suit. This indecision is an act you're putting on to buy time...
It's not easy to outfox a fox, is it?
("Project Dawn"... I assume she's referring to those documents the Commander retrieved from the Northern Parliament by the same name...)
...Whoever came up with your codename is a very clever person.
Heehee. I'll be sure to forward your compliment to them.
Hey, relax. I swear on my position on the Council that my next question isn't so bad.
Don't overthink it. Just answer how your heart tells you.
...No comment, then.
Question number six it is, then. It's also the final question.
You've answered three of my questions thus far, so you cannot pass on this last one.
I promised I wouldn't be mean with it, but you also have to answer it no matter what... Heehee. Let me mull it over for a moment.
Constellation could not believe her ears.
Silver Fox had been so calm and composed, and now a worrying tremble ran through her words.
Indeed, her voice sounded like she was trying and failing to mask her excitement.
(Something tells me her all other questions were only setting the stage... This is the one she REALLY wanted to ask.)
I'll... answer your question as best as I can, Miss.
Okay, little girl. Here is my final question.
How are things in Sverdlovsk?
...What?
Constellation imagined dozens of questions, dozens of motives, dozens of answers. None of them prepared her for this last question.
It felt so off the cuff she couldn't help but flinch.
Uhh... That's a place in the Northern Parliament, right?
Sorry, but I know next to nothing about their geography.
I don't know where it is, much less the latest goings-on there...
I see...
Even through the compression, Constellation could deeply feel the disappointment and sadness in Silver Fox's voice.
...Say, is that place where you come from?
Then let's say that one didn't count. Ask another question.
...Little girl, your world's secrets are in the balance here. It's unwise to be so generous.
No, it's fine. This is just a little quiz between us.
You should be able to bend the rules slightly for everyone to get something out of it.
Well, if you're certain. Then I'll ask something else.
The sound coming from the communicator cut off for a moment.
...I've come up with a new question. Here goes.
Is Aoste still doing well?
...Aoste? Who?
Sorry, I've never heard that name before.
This one doesn't count either. Ask another one!
Constellation waited... and waited... and waited, but there was no response from the communicator.
...Hello? Ms. Silver Fox?
Hey, everyone. I've brought your medal.
And I have some good news, too!
Sorry if I'm interrupting. Is the quiz over already?
Well...
We're done. What good news do you bring, Princeton?
The beacon – it's been repaired.
Someone had meddled with its memory banks and injected malware.
Luckily, it has been removed. All that remains is to activate the beacon and extract all the data logs inside.
Great! If that little bugger is the reason we got sent here, it should contain some clue that'll help us get home again!
What are we waiting for? Let's power it up!
Shall I, Ms. Silver Fox?
Yes. Start it.
With Silver Fox's permission, Princeton put her finger on the beacon's power button.
She gave it a gentle press, and...
Just like that, everything and everyone was gone.
The Council's fleet, the ruined sea, Fortress, the other Princeton...
Even their surroundings vanished, and in their place, a familiar sight.
Isn't this the Coral Sea simulation?
Are we... back?
What just happened? Their whole fleet vanished in the twinkle of an eye...
That beacon probably had something to do with it.
I've found Princeton...
Which one?
Ours, Guam! She's over there... unconscious... Waaahhhh!
Princeton! Talk to us!
Zzz... Saratoga... Alaska... Commander... You shouldn't have prepared such a big welcoming party just for me...
Heehee... Zzz...
Thank goodness. She's just asleep.
And having pretty sweet dreams, from the sound of it.
Poor girl isn't gonna like reality when she wakes up, though. No Saratoga and no Commander.
But a party? We can make that happen! Let her sleep while we make all the arrangements!
(She's asked four questions about the moon's soil, the Sea of Stars, Wisdom Cubes, and the Sirens. We've answered three of them.)
I feel bad that I couldn't answer her final question...
Don't sweat it. I'm sure her data is somewhere among our simulations. You can answer it the next time you meet.
When the people want a second season you can bet there'll be one. It's the same with this.
Plus, my gut tells me we will see each other again!
...Let's hope so.
BEEP BEEP!
This is TB. I have picked up your signals.
Report your current status. My readings indicate that you are in the Coral Sea simulation once again. Please confirm.
Yeah, we're here and we're fine! We'll explain everything that happened later!
I'm so tired... You do the explaining this time, Guam...
Ahahah, alrighty then. TB, what happened on your end?
There was a hostile program among the data salvaged. It has been removed. The Galactic Core is back to operating as normal.
Initiating simulation exit sequence now.
Finally! Did you hear that? We're going home!
Mhm. I need a long nap when I get back.
For once, I might just spend the night sleeping instead of stargazing... *yaaawn*...
Somewhere, in an unknown location...
...The light of a beacon.
I was too late...

Looking Back.say: Sea of Stars - Galactic Core Common Area
Oh! You're back!
Looking Back.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Looking Back.say: In one piece, too, baby!
Did you and TB manage the crisis from the control room?
Yeah!
Looking Back.factiontag: Advanced KAN-SEN Research and Planning Department

Looking Back.say: Anchorage's hard work helped me deal with the incident swiftly.
Well done. That is worth a gold star.
Analyzing the data that has been salvaged is going to take time.
Once it has been verified to be completely safe, it will be sent to the Testing Department.
Alright. Thanks.
Wait, when did we salvage data? That beacon disappeared before we could analyze it.
Flasher and I did it.
Yup. Stella and I were marking important targets and gathering data as we went!
What happened to the rule about no photography or filming?
The rule only applies to those two. Data salvaging is perfectly fine.
Someone will need to file a report detailing the events of what transpired.
Oh, right. I'd almost forgotten.
Mmgh... I can't do it. I've been up for too long... Zzz...
Well, she's out, so I suppose I'll have to write the analysis report.
It pains me to say that I passed out halfway through the mission...
I missed everything that happened in that purified sea...
Then Flasher and Laffey are gonna have to– Uh, where did they go?
They bailed! Don't tell me I have to write that mission report?!
You can do it... Zzz...
NOOOOOOOO!

A Message Arrives.say: I check my inbox and see a mail from the Sea of Stars. It's an accident report.
"Accident" could mean anything... I hope it was nothing too bad.
...During a data salvage operation, three teams were transported to an unknown simulation, where we encountered an organization called "the Council."
That emblem... I'm pretty sure I saw it in the Arcana Sanctum with Belorussiya.
So that facility must have been one of theirs.
...Their leader went by the codename "Silver Fox" and knew who Dr. Aoste was.
Interesting. I wonder if both Anzeel and Aoste were fellow members of this "Council."
Since they clearly knew me when we spoke, I might also have been a member.
...A lone Antiochus specimen named "War Protocol Fortress."
That's a new model of Siren – or should I say, Antiochus.
Wisdom Cube limiters, a way to control METAmorphosis...
Nope, no pieces are falling into place in my head.
I suppose new information won't always trigger flashbacks like I've had before.
Come to think of it, the flashbacks have always happened when I'm in a Mirror Sea or in one of the Sea of Stars' simulations...
They were even more frequent when I was hooked up to the Reality Lens...
Up next, the question the Devil raised... "Where are the Sea of Stars' simulations?"
It is odd, and to the best of my knowledge, the Galactic Core didn't malfunction.
The existence of this "Council" and their mysterious fleet... might be the key to everything.
Ideally, I'd like to speak with them face to face.
If I ever do, I'm going to ask them how that beacon came to be infected with abnormal data.
Finally, there's the black tornado, or what they called a "corrosion phenomenon." The Northern Parliament and Royal Navy have both reported similar sightings.
...That beacon cannot be allowed to be restored. We'll halt the remaining simulations.
It's time to shift our focus to developing Type II riggings. We've gathered more than enough testing data to do it.

Preparing for a Picnic.say: I've received a report summary from my servant through my private channel. There is no risk of Naval HQ finding out, so you should read it too.
My word... They encountered a corrosion phenomenon in the Sea of Stars?
This means our prediction has come true. The corrosion has reached your branch now.
Thank goodness your servant is so communicative and willing to share confidential matters with you.
Unlike a certain someone who refuses to share much of anything.
Shut up. To mock me is to mock yourself. We both know that knowing too much is not a good thing.
I've had it. Renovate these remaining areas yourself.
I don't know what to do with them, and it's you who's going to use them anyway.
But they won't be done in a day, and there's one more important thing we have to do.
What do you say we have a picnic, hmm?
It's time we introduced Ms. D to you, and a little outing is a great way to do it.

Cold Steel, Twice Broken.say: Sovetsky Soyuz lay silently on a machine at the center of a large chamber.
Her rigging was stripped down to its base, its shark-like heads having been detached along with all removable auxiliary weapons.
Soobrazitelny, Chkalov, what are the test results?
...The same, in the end. Damage present, cause unknown.
I was so sure it was going to succeed this time...
There were no issues whatsoever during testing, yet here we are. It boggles my genius mind!
Hmm. You did directly engage Gazers during the World Expo incident, Comrade Soyuz.
They must've inflicted unrepairable damage to you, just like long ago.
Alternatively, you were born with a limitation on your hull. A limitation even I am unable to identify.
Whatever it is, I think it renders you incapable of using a new rigging. You are stuck with your old one.
I think Ms. Mechanic here is onto something, even if there's nothing to support her theory.
Without knowing the root of the issue, there's nothing we can do. I say we instead focus on improving the material of your weaponry.
Let's hope the research team in the Antarctic will have a breakthrough.
I trust you with the research side of things.
At the very least, I'll be able to weather the stress of regular combat with these new modifications.
The world is in a precarious situation and I need to regain my fighting ability as soon as possible.

Outer Defensive Line.say: Location unknown - Outer defensive line
This was the outer perimeter, where the Lovers and the Empress had erected a defensive line against X's impending assault.
Countless interceptor weapons filled the sky, accompanied by floating fortresses as far as the eye could see.
From land, to sea, to even the heavens, a curtain of steel rose high.
It was like a hive of epic proportions, ANTI-X's fleets moving to and fro between defensive structures like worker bees.
There were humanoid units on patrol, warships of every size, massive ships for moving cargo, swarms of drones and unmanned aircraft...
This massive orchestra with guns in place of instruments was conducted silently by one girl, her skin pale and her eyes emotionless.
Reflected in those eyes was a horizon, and a dark audience. Her gaze was unflinchingly focused on it.
Ahah! It's been a while since you brought out the good stuff, ANTI-X.
Is this all for little old me?
...Category V threat approaching. Response: Eliminate.
Aww. I wanted to reminisce for a bit first.
Come on. Didn't you see the light from that beacon?
I did. It reminded me of the old days.
I sent a few gifts. What about you?
...That has already been dealt with.
Your battle is here. With me.
And with you, no less! I don't think I've had the pleasure before. You must be very confident in yourself.
Let me guess – your plan is to hold out as long as possible, hoping you'll win a battle of attrition. Am I right?
Heheh. I've got a fun fact for you.
I'm actually very, very patient.

sequence: This is Fortress. Correspondence attempt number 3122.
"Say, what do you think that all was?"
It was not. I am confident in saying that.
"You're right. The cola they gave us is still in the freezer."
"I went to bring a bring a bottle to Ms. Silver Fox. Do you know what she said to me?"
"She said... Princeton, do you believe in fate?"
"It was so out of left field I couldn't believe it."
"...Fortress, we will see our home again, won't we?"

グッドモーニング星の海.say: カメラセッティング……OK
パラメーター設定……OK
録画モード……Start！
「星の海」で頑張っているみなさんおはようございます！時刻は朝4時10分になりまし……
うーん、4時におはようって言うのはちょっとヘンな気がするけど…
Anyway～この番組が見られてる頃にはもう朝だし！
おはようでOK！問題ナッシング！
…いや、でもよく考えたら、もし何かトラブルが起きて配信時間がズレたら…やっぱり明日の朝に予約配信したほうが良くない？
本末転倒ね！NG！…はい、カットカットカット！
改めて！「星の海」で頑張っているみなさんおはようございまぁす！
みなさんおなじみのユニオン巡洋艦CB-2、美少女艦船タレントのグアムでーす！
今の時刻は朝4時10分！普段忙しい格納庫はガラガラで、おばけでも出てきそうな雰囲気！
そう！勘のいい方はもうお分かりかと思いますが～
「グアムはまさか施設のおばけの噂をとことん調べる気？！」
Exactly！その通りでございまーす♪
ユニオン研究施設、「星の海」におばけが出る噂をまだ知らない方もいると思いますが、実は――
グアム、私たちは番組撮影ではなく、仲間の出迎えに来たでしょ？
それにおばけが出る噂なんて、全然聞いたことがないよ
言われてみればそうだった！グアムたちがここにいるのは仲間を出迎えるため！
そして「星の海」のおばけ騒ぎは全くのデマ！みなさん、科学の力を信じましょう！くれぐれも嘘を信じないように！
その言い方だと逆に疑いたくなるような…
わざとこういう言い方にしたよ♪
あ。連絡機がそろそろ到着するみたいよ。カメラをオフにしたほうがいいんじゃない？
ダメだよノーザンプトン！カメラをここに設置したのはまさに連絡機が到着した瞬間を撮るためだよ
サン・ジャシントに、プリンストンがここに来る様子を撮って欲しいって頼まれたからね
「いつも真面目なプリンストンがワクワクしてる姿が見たい」んだって
まあ別にただでやってあげるわけじゃないからね
代わりにコンステレーションが開催する関係者ミーティングの予定を教えてもらったもの♪今日は絶対に出たくないからね！
…………今のはコンステレーションに聞かれたらまずいんじゃない？
へーきへーき！あとで編集するときにばっさりカットするから
しーっ。なにか聞こえない？もう連絡機が到着したのかな？
マップアプリによると……ここを通るみたい
格納庫がこんなに広いなんて…山の中の施設だと聞いたけど、いくらなんでも大きすぎるよ…
それで…次はここから降りて…？それとも目の前の廊下を？うぅ、なんだか迷路に迷い込んだ気分…
ラフィーちゃん、私にしっかりついてきてね。はぐれて迷子になったら大変なんだから
うん……
にしても、これほどのメガストラクチャーをどうやって建てたの……
しかもこの壁の材質…もしかしたら外では全く使われていないなにかだったりして…
うぅ、やっぱり迷いそう…誰かに案内してもらわないと…
前に誰かいるみたい？あのー
って、ルイビルじゃない！あなたも「星の海」に配属されたの？
プリンストンとラフィーちゃん…？どうかしました…？
ええと、ヘルクレス座…「兵装設計部」に行きたいんだけど…
ごめんなさい。実は私もここに配属されたばかりで、行き方が分からなくて

窓がなく照明もつけていない漆黒の部屋に、プラネタリウムの星の光が天井に散らばっている。
頭上の銀河を見上げながら、机の前の椅子に座っている少女は囁く。
どんなに星占いをしても結果は同じ……
今度こそうまくいく……

擬似空間「珊瑚海」――任務エリア
月の光で銀色に照らされた霧が、果てしない海をヴェールのように覆っている。
島々の間から吹き抜ける潮風が懐かしい暖かさを帯びるも、その感触は儚い。
過去と未来、真実と虚偽が交錯する「再現」の戦場。確かな記憶と見知らぬ気配が入り交り、見る者に眩暈すら感じさせる景色を織りなす――

もう一つの可能性.say: ホワイトアウトした視界がもとに戻り、コンステレーションの前には氷雪に覆われた白い世界が広がった。
水平線…地平線のその先に、金属と氷で構成された、天に届きそうなほどの巨大な構造物が佇んでいる。

通信が回復したらTBが連絡してくるはずだから、まずはこのあたりを調査しよう
ここの「王冠」は私たちが知っている「王冠」とは違うみたい
そうですね…中にエネルギーが渦巻いていて、まるで「生きている」ように見えます
だから変なんだよ
ほら、私たちが知っている「王冠」は起動されなかった特異点だよ
ロイヤルと鉄血で繰り広げられた「再現」でヴィクトリアスがコード「G」に出会った
あとで判明したけど、あの時コード「G」は王冠を起動しようとしたけど、セイレーンのピュリファイアーに止められている
その後「王冠」はずっとセイレーンに抑えられたまま…
そのはずなのに……
ここの「王冠」はちゃんと動いている…
この擬似空間は「珊瑚海」から回収された異常データを元にしていますから、私たちの知っている「王冠」と同一存在とは限りませんよ
それなら尚更まずいかもしれない
私の知らない間に、もう一つの「王冠」が存在する可能性があるとでも言うの？
…で、でもなぜ今まで私たちだけでなく北方連合も気づかなかったの？
もし「王冠」が動いているなら、鏡面海域ですらそれほどのエネルギーを隠し通すことはできないはず…
動いて…動いていない……今は動いていないのに、動いている……
「王冠」は鉄血の「ミッドガルドの塔」と同じく、人工特異点のようなものとかですかね？
今「王冠」は動いていませんけど、実は私たちの知らない間に「王冠」は一度起動しているとか…
このデータはその時の「王冠」の起動を記録したものだとでもいうの…？
筋は通っている。可能性はあるね。…でも確証はまだ持てない…
「王冠」の監視は各陣営それぞれでしているから見逃すはずがないし、それに仮に鏡面海域で隠すとしても起動する時にエネルギーが外に漏れ出すはず
あと考えられる可能性は、そもそも「王冠」が起動したのは各陣営の監視体制が出来る前から……
でも、「珊瑚海」の擬似空間になぜこんなデータがあるの？
まさか異常データがタイムトラベルかパラレルワールドから――
静寂の夜空に爆ぜた轟音は、コンステレーションの推理を中断させた。
……な、何の音？！
あれは……コンステレーション、見て！黒い竜巻よ…！
悪意が形を成した災いが「王冠」の向こうに突如現れた。
最初の轟音が収まってまもなく、水天の境界線から黒い壁が立ちはだかった。
空を、海を覆う闇雲が急速に広がり、「王冠」の中心部にあるエネルギーの輝きを色褪せさせた。
む、向かってきている…！？
逃げよう！ジャシント！早くここから逃げないと――
一方、「星の海」制御室――
警告：リアリティレンズに敵性データ発生
警告：隔離シーケンス、起動失敗しました
警告：防御システムの負荷がまもなく臨界値に到達します。敵性データが拡散する恐れがあります
警告します――
警報の音が鳴り響き、制御室中が無数のエラー表示によって赤く染まっている。
ああああ！あ、あかくなってる……もにたー、あかくなってる……！
